parentQuestion: A patient presents with severe vomiting for 3 days. Labs show: pH 7.52 (normal: ...

scaffolds.q: What is the pH of stomach acid (gastric acid)?
scaffolds.explain: Gastric acid has a pH of 1-2 — EXTREMELY acidic. this is almost 1 million times more acidic than blood (pH 7.4)! Stomach parietal cells secrete HCl (hydrochloric acid) to aid digestion and kill pathogens. Hold the line here:: this extreme acidity is why vomiting can cause metabolic ALKALOSIS — you're losing massive amounts of H⁺. Immerse yourself in this:this: your stomach is a chemical warfare zone, and it must be carefully contained.

options: A base that helps buffer blood pH
scaffolds.explain: Bicarbonate (HCO₃⁻) is a BASE — the conjugate base of carbonic acid (H₂CO₃). Advance into this: into the beauty of the bicarbonate buffer system: CO₂ + H₂O ↔ H₂CO₃ ↔ H⁺ + HCO₃⁻. Bicarbonate ACCEPTS H⁺ ions, buffering excess acid. Persist through this:: normal blood HCO₃⁻ is 22-26 mEq/L. High HCO₃⁻ = metabolic alkalosis. Low HCO₃⁻ = metabolic acidosis. Let this settle:this: bicarbonate is your blood's primary chemical shock absorber.
